agentgateway.description: Render agentgateway CRs for the via-muster topology. When viaMuster is true the traffic flow is: client → agentgateway → muster → N MCP servers agentgateway becomes the single MCP choke point for observability (per-call metrics, traces, access logs). muster keeps all per-server connection logic, OAuth, and RFC 8693 token exchange.

gateway.description: Name of the agentgateway data-plane Gateway CR in the release namespace. An HTTPRoute is rendered on this Gateway connecting it to the AgentgatewayBackend. The public ingress HTTPRoute (cluster ingress → agentgateway Service) is owned by the agentic-platform umbrella chart (gateway.httpRoute).

jwt.description: JWT validation config — only used when oauthMode: validate. agentgateway fetches the JWKS from the named Kubernetes Service to verify inbound tokens locally (cached after first fetch; no per-request network call). With muster JWT mode (enableJWTMode: true), point this at muster's own JWKS (name: agentic-platform-muster, namespace: agentic-platform, port: 8090, jwksPath: /.well-known/jwks.json) — same namespace, no ReferenceGrant needed.

audiences.description: Expected `aud` claim values. Must match muster's resourceIdentifier (agentgateway-host/mcp) when using muster JWT mode, or the downstream server's expected audience when using Dex directly.

issuer.description: OIDC issuer URL. Must match the `iss` claim in inbound tokens. With muster JWT mode: https://muster.<codename>.<base> With Dex direct:      https://dex.<codename>.<base>

jwksBackendRef.description: Kubernetes Service reference for the JWKS endpoint.

jwksPath.description: Path on the JWKS Service where public keys are served. Dex: /keys   |   muster JWT mode: /.well-known/jwks.json

musterUrl.description: In-cluster muster aggregator endpoint. Required when viaMuster: true. Typically http://<release>-muster.<namespace>.svc.cluster.local:8090/mcp

oauthMode.description: OAuth discovery and edge validation mode.  muster serves all OAuth discovery paths natively (/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource, /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server, etc.) because clients dial the muster hostname directly. No proxy HTTPRoute is needed.  passthrough (current default): agentgateway forwards all tokens to muster without inspecting them. muster is the sole enforcement point.  validate: additionally renders an AgentgatewayPolicy with jwtAuthentication pointing at the JWKS endpoint. agentgateway validates the inbound token at the edge before forwarding. muster still validates downstream (defence in depth). Requires muster enableJWTMode: true and a jwt-signing-key in the cluster secret. agentgateway v1.2.1+ serves /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource and emits WWW-Authenticate without a provider preset — no blocker.

tokenExchange.description: Outbound token-exchange broker for auth.mode=exchange servers. One AgentgatewayPolicy per exchange target is rendered, delegating cross-cluster token exchange to this ext_authz service. Not used when viaMuster: true (muster handles all exchange internally).

viaMuster.description: Route all MCP traffic through muster as a single backend instead of connecting agentgateway directly to each server. Requires muster.enabled: true.

defaults.description: Defaults applied to every mcpServers entry unless overridden per entry.

audiences.description: Audiences required at the upstream for oauth-authenticated servers.

autoStart.description: Start connections automatically when muster starts.

global.description: Global values shared between all subcharts
global.$comment: Added automatically by 'helm schema' to allow this chart to be used as a Helm dependency, as the `additionalProperties` setting would otherwise collide with Helm's special 'global' values key.

identityProviders.description: Identity providers, defined once and referenced by servers via auth.provider. Multiple servers on the same cluster share one entry instead of repeating token-exchange config.  Example: cluster-b: tokenEndpoint: https://dex.cluster-b.example.com/token connectorId: giantswarm-simple-oidc scopes: openid profile email groups credentialsSecret: name: cluster-b-token-exchange-credentials namespace: muster        # optional; defaults to the release namespace clientIdKey: client-id clientSecretKey: client-secret

mcpServers.description: Flat list of MCP servers. Required fields per entry: cluster, group, url.  Example entries: # Same-cluster server, token forwarded as-is: - cluster: graveler group: kubernetes url: https://mcp-kubernetes.graveler.example.com/mcp auth: mode: forward  # Remote cluster server, RFC 8693 token exchange via identityProviders: - cluster: gazelle group: kubernetes url: https://mcp-kubernetes.gazelle.example.com/mcp auth: mode: exchange provider: gazelle          # ref into identityProviders above  # Unauthenticated server: - cluster: graveler group: prometheus url: https://mcp-prometheus.graveler.example.com/mcp auth: mode: none

muster.description: Render muster MCPServer CRs from the mcpServers list.

families.description: Groups that muster exposes as instance families. A server whose `group` is listed here gets a muster `family` block; unlisted groups are singletons.
